page: Amazon Web Services m9g.large is a general-purpose instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features the ARMv9 architecture with the Neoverse-V3 CPU model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) with 1.0 thread per core, backed by 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results show a divergence between single-threaded and multi-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded operations, with gzip compression reaching 36.54 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.3 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is comparatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3984.9 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 34.1 GB/sec. Memory performance is mixed; the Passmark memory score is strong at 2799.13, and large-block read-only memory bandwidth is average at 65.44 GB/sec, but write bandwidth and small-block read bandwidth fall into the weak category.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m9g.large offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high network throughput. It is suited for single-threaded tasks, basic database operations, Redis key-value stores, and static web serving. It is less suitable for multi-threaded compute-heavy tasks, cryptography, or large language model inference, where it demonstrates poor to weak performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz, 8.0 GB of memory, and a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks reveal a strong contrast: single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression achieve top-tier speeds, while multi-core performance and cryptography tests rank as weak or poor. Memory performance is mixed, with a strong Passmark memory score but weak write bandwidth. This instance is suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and Redis key-value operations, but exhibits clear performance tradeoffs in multi-threaded compute and encryption tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and strong single-threaded performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and balanced memory capacity for lightweight web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores at 2.8 GHz and 8.0 GB of RAM
Delivers top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
Provides 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Exhibits weak multi-core scaling and poor cryptographic benchmark performance
Suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and single-threaded tasks
